briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. Welcome to the climate briefing for April 15, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 The Reality of the Climate Crisis and Urgent Warnings
sections.content: News collected today clearly illustrates the direct and severe impacts of the climate crisis on our lives. Domestically, abnormal weather conditions have led to a phenomenon where bees that leave to collect nectar do not return, putting the beekeeping industry in crisis [4]. Consequently, local governments, such as Gwanak-gu, are being called upon to take direct action, including establishing the 3rd Climate Crisis Adaptation Plan to protect residents in the face of the materialized climate crisis [7]. Internationally, global warming is intensifying the most powerful hurricanes, and threats to social infrastructure are escalating, with a report released in Minnesota indicating that climate change is jeopardizing infrastructure [55, 58]. Furthermore, new research suggesting that the likelihood of a collapse of the Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation (AMOC) is much higher than previously thought warns of the instability of the global climate system [75]. There are also warnings that climate change is being identified as a major cause of global health threats, leading to new health issues such as an increased risk of Arenavirus fever [67, 72].

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic Carbon Neutrality Practices and Efforts to Expand Renewable Energy
sections.content: Domestic efforts to address the climate crisis are being driven by the active participation of various stakeholders. Chungbuk Province and Yongin City are operating 'Climate Change Week' in conjunction with Earth Day, promoting carbon neutrality practices [1, 2]. Gwangmyeong City is accelerating its progress towards 2050 carbon neutrality by providing awards to apartments excelling in energy saving and carbon neutrality practices [10, 11, 12, 22]. Corporations are also declaring carbon neutrality goals and implementing ESG management; Yogiyo and We Great Imagination are pursuing electric vehicle conversion and expanding reusable container deliveries with a target of carbon neutrality by 2050 [18, 20, 21, 26]. In the renewable energy sector, the government has announced renewable energy as one of the four mega-special zone initiatives, alongside robotics, bio-tech, and AI autonomous vehicles, and is discussing regulatory exemptions [28, 29, 35, 37, 39]. Legislation for agrivoltaics is also gaining momentum, raising expectations for increased farm income and energy security [36]. Regional-level efforts to expand eco-friendly energy are also active, with Jinan County conducting a demand survey for the promotion of new and renewable energy convergence support projects [32, 33, 43].

sections.title: 🌍 International Cooperation and Promotion of Climate Change Awareness
sections.content: International cooperation and efforts to raise public awareness regarding climate change continue. Prime Minister Kim attended the Asia Carbon Neutrality Online Summit, where he discussed energy countermeasures and supply chain policy coordination in light of the Middle East conflict, urging ASEAN nations to reduce their reliance on fossil fuels and increase the proportion of renewable energy [9, 14, 15, 42]. In the education sector, the Jeonnam Provincial Office of Education Training Institute is running 'Climate Change Response and Ecological Transition Education' [3, 6], and Incheon is hosting a children's carbon neutrality art exhibition, striving to cultivate climate awareness among future generations [19, 24]. Internationally, Jeff Bezos has made a significant private contribution by donating 10 billion dollars to climate change initiatives [44]. Various cultural and educational endeavors are ongoing to encourage the public to rethink climate change, including through the Oregon Museum of Science and Industry (OMSI) and the NYU Earth Climate Change Film Festival [49, 51, 60, 69]. Efforts to strengthen the scientific foundation are also underway, with the University of Maryland embarking on the development of a U.S. Greenhouse Gas Inventory and Analysis [65].

sections.title: ⚠️ Policy Challenges and the Shadow of Energy Transition
sections.content: Despite efforts to address climate change, policy challenges and obstacles in the energy transition process continue to cast a significant shadow. Domestically, issues with grid connection for achieving the 100GW renewable energy target have arisen, and related legislative improvements are adrift, hindering the expansion of renewable energy [27]. Internationally, some policy setbacks are observed, such as the Trump administration's Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) overturning a landmark climate change ruling [53], and Delta Airlines following the U.S. government in abandoning key climate change goals [64]. Furthermore, the near absence of climate change in popular culture, including films, adds difficulty to raising public awareness of the crisis [52]. Meanwhile, an analysis revealing that the world's top 100 oil and gas companies gained over 30 million dollars per hour in undue profits during the first month of the U.S.-Israel war highlights the contradictory reality of immense profit generation by the fossil fuel industry in an era of climate crisis [76].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlights the multifaceted aspects of the climate crisis and the urgent need for global efforts to address it. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.original_title: Critical Atlantic current significantly more likely to collapse than thought
articles.description: Scientists say finding is ‘very concerning’ as collapse would be catastrophic for Europe, Africa and the AmericasThe critical Atlantic current system appears significantly more likely to collapse than previously thought after new research found that climate models predicting the biggest slowdown are the most realistic. Scientists called the new finding “very concerning” as a collapse would have catastrophic consequences for Europe, Africa and the Americas.The Atlantic meridional overturning circ

summary.phenomenon: The critical Atlantic current system (Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation) appears significantly more likely to collapse than previously thought, based on new research indicating that climate models predicting the greatest slowdown are the most realistic.
summary.outlook: The collapse of this current system would have catastrophic consequences for Europe, Africa, and the Americas.

articles.title: $30m an hour: big oil reaping huge war windfall from consumers, analysis finds
articles.description: Exclusive: Climate action blockers including Saudi Arabia, Russia and major fossil fuel firms set to make extra $234bn by end of 2026Middle East crisis – live updatesThe world’s top 100 oil and gas companies banked more than $30m every hour in unearned profit in the first month of the US-Israeli war in Iran, according to exclusive analysis for the Guardian. Saudi Aramco, Gazprom and ExxonMobil are among the biggest beneficiaries of the bonanza, meaning key opponents of climate action continue to

summary.phenomenon: The world's top 100 oil and gas companies banked more than $30 million per hour in unearned profit during the first month of the US-Israeli war.

summary.outlook: Climate action blockers, including Saudi Arabia, Russia, and major fossil fuel firms, are projected to earn an additional $234 billion by the end of 2026, indicating that key opponents of climate action continue to profit.
